message:
feat(upgrade): add `cass upgrade` CLI subcommand for self-update (#200)

Closes #200. The self-update machinery in update_check.rs (GitHub
release check, SHA256-verified installer, run_self_update) was already
fully implemented but only reachable via the y/N prompt that fires
when `cass tui` launches — useless for headless / SSH / scripted
environments.

This patch wires it as a first-class subcommand:

    cass upgrade [--check] [--force] [--yes] [--json]

| flag              | behaviour                                             |
|-------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|
| (none)            | check, prompt y/N, run installer                      |
| --check           | print versions, exit 1 if update available else 0     |
| --force           | bypass 1-hour cadence, re-fetch the GitHub API        |
| --yes / -y        | skip the prompt, install non-interactively            |
| --json / --robot  | structured output (combines naturally with --check)   |

`--check` and `--yes` are mutually exclusive (clap-enforced). When the
caller is non-interactive (`!stdin().is_terminal()`) and didn't pass
`--yes`, the command refuses to install and prints exactly which flag
to add — the previous failure mode would have been a silent prompt
hang in CI.

Implementation is mostly plumbing on top of existing primitives:

- New `Commands::Upgrade { check, force, yes, json }` variant.
- New `run_upgrade(check_only, force, yes, output_format)` that
  delegates to `update_check::force_check` (when --force) or
  `update_check::check_for_updates` (default), then either prints /
  exits 1 (--check), prompts (default), or runs `run_self_update`
  (--yes or accepted prompt).
- Updated `command_name_for_telemetry` and `is_robot_mode` so the
  subcommand registers correctly with the rest of the CLI plumbing.

Edge cases handled:

- No UpdateInfo (cadence skip / network failure / disabled) — explain
  why on stderr instead of pretending we're up to date; --json mode
  emits {"checked": false, "reason": "..."} so wrappers can
  distinguish "no update" from "couldn't check".
- Already on latest — informational message, exit 0.
- Update available + structured output + --check — emit JSON, exit 1
  (matches the documented contract).

Verification:
- cargo build clean
- cargo test --lib: 4128 passed / 0 failed / 3 ignored
- `cass upgrade --help` lists all four flags with the documented
  semantics.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
